{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|Name=Mishima Shrine
|画像={{ill|File:三島神社(下谷).jpg|ja|三島神社(下谷).jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:三島神社(下谷).jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|所在地=Tokyo, Taito Ward, Shimotani 3-chome, 7-5, ({{sfn|東京都神社庁}})
|祭神={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
|社格=Village Shrine
|創建={{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}4th year （{{ill|1281|en|1281|WD=Q5537|lt=1281|1=1281}}）
|例祭={{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}}・{{ill|June 15|en|June 15|WD=Q2613|lt=15|1=June 15}} on the Saturday and Sunday nearest these dates
}}

'''Mishima Shrine''' (三島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in 3-chome, {{ill|Shitaya|en|Shitaya|WD=Q11361329|lt=Shimotani|1=Shitaya}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taito Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Its head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ooyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in Imabari, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}—situated in the Ichinomiya of {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}—which is revered as Japan’s National Guardian Shrine.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deity  
** {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
* Auxiliary Deities  
** {{ill|Watasuhiko|ja|和足彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Mihajimahime|ja|身島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihajimahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Kami-tsuhime|ja|上津姫命|lt=Thunder God|lt_ja=雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-tsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Shimo-tsuhime|ja|下津姫命|lt=Dragon God|lt_ja=龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-tsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
Kami-tsuhime is identified with {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}}, and Shimo-tsuhime is identified with {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}.

== History ==
In the 4th year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} ({{ill|1281|en|1281|WD=Q5537|lt=1281|1=1281}}), {{ill|Kōno Michiyuki|ja|河野通有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Michiyuki|1=Kawano Michiari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554275}} set out to pray for victory in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol Invasion|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}} by appealing to his tutelary deity, Mishima Daimyojin ({{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ooyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}). Under the guidance of the white heron—the divine messenger ({{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=Divine Messenger|1=Shinshi}}) of Mishima Daimyojin—he achieved remarkable military exploits. Upon his return, he received in a dream the divine command to enshrine Mishima Daimyojin in the {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}’s {{ill|Toyoshima District|ja|豊島郡 (武蔵国)|lt=Toyoshima District|lt_ja=豊島郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima District|1=Toshima district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10876926}}, and it is said that he subsequently enshrined the deity at the residence of the Kōno clan within Ueno Mountain ({{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Mountain|1=Ueno Park}}).

In the 3rd year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}} ({{ill|1650|en|1650|WD=Q6951|lt=1650|1=1650}}), he was ordered by {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} to relocate the shrine, and it was moved to Kanasusimura in Taito Ward (presently in {{ill|Negishi|ja|根岸 (台東区)|lt=Negishi|lt_ja=根岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negishi|1=Negishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536292}}). Furthermore, in the 7th year of {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}), when the shrine grounds became property of the shogunate, it was relocated to Asakusa Koayacho (currently in {{ill|Kotobukicho|ja|寿 (台東区)|lt=Kotobukicho|lt_ja=寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobukicho|1=Kotobuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458280}})—namely, the head shrine, Head Shrine Mishima Shrine, in Shijō-chō of Kotobukicho. However, as the tutelary deity was too distant, a branch spirit was enshrined at the Hiyoke Inari Shrine site in Kanasukimachi of Kanasusimura. This branch is known as the Mishima Shrine of Kanasukimachi (Shimotani). To this day, the successive head priests of this shrine have been descendants of Kōno Michiyuki.

In addition, parishioners in Negishi of Kanasusimura also enshrined a branch spirit from the head shrine and co-enshrined it at Kumano Shrine; this is the Former Mishima Shrine of Negishi 1-chome.

== Shrine Grounds ==
* **Hiyoke Inari Shrine** – A subsidiary shrine ({{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}) within the grounds, serving as the land deity ({{ill|Landlord deity|en|Landlord deity|WD=Q4160872|lt=Land Deity|1=Landlord deity}}) of Mishima Shrine. Its date of establishment is unknown, but it is said that the Tokugawa clan built it within Ueno Mountain for protection against fires. In ancient times, it was known as “Misaki Inari.” Later, as the area was designated as a {{ill|Fire Protection Zone|ja|火除地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Protection Zone|1=hiyokechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566980}}, it came to be known as “Hiyoke Inari” and has since been venerated for its powers to ward off fires, bring prosperity in business, ensure family safety, prevent disasters, and secure overall safety.
* **Raijin Well** – In old Musashino, where lightning was frequent and greatly feared by the locals, lightning once struck Mishima Shrine. The priest sealed the lightning in a well. When the lightning implored, “Release me from the well,” the priest granted its request on the condition that it never strike the area again. It is said that lightning has not struck here since, and the well is revered as a talisman against lightning.
* **Mishima Shrine Hana-hyō Monument** – A monument inscribed by {{ill|Yukio Ozaki|en|Yukio Ozaki|WD=Q1152666|lt=Ozaki Kyūdō|1=Yukio Ozaki}} during his tenure as Mayor of Tokyo.
* **Survey Point** – A surveying marker established in the early Meiji era by the Geographic Division of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}}, marked by an inscription of “Fu” on the paving stone at the north gate entrance.
* Saplings of the {{ill|Miharu Takizakura|en|Miharu Takizakura|WD=Q3132219|lt=Miharu Takizakura|1=Miharu Takizakura}} in {{ill|Miharu, Fukushima|en|Miharu, Fukushima|WD=Q1348269|lt=Miharu Town|1=Miharu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.

== Reconstruction Plan ==
In proceeding with the reconstruction of the aging shrine buildings, a portion of the property will be subject to a fixed-term land lease ({{ill|Emphyteusis|en|Emphyteusis|WD=Q690076|lt=Fixed-term Land Lease|1=Emphyteusis}}) until {{ill|2099|en|2099|WD=Q972339|lt=2099|1=2099}},<ref name="tatekae1">{{cite web |title=Mitsui Fudosan’s Integrated Development Condominium with Shrine in Iriyatani |author= |agency=|publisher=Real Estate Distribution Research Institute|url=https://www.re-port.net/article/news/0000077259/|date=2024-11-11|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> and {{ill|Mitsui Fudosan Residential|ja|三井不動産レジデンシャル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Fudosan Residential|1=Mitsui Fudosan Residential|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354219}} is developing a condominium ({{ill|Condominium|en|Condominium|WD=Q161851|lt=Condominium|1=Condominium}}). Furthermore, there is an initiative to repurpose some of the roof tiles ({{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|WD=Q268547|lt=Roof Tile|1=Roof tiles}}) and lanterns ({{ill|Lantern|ja|灯篭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}) used before reconstruction as common materials or art in the condominium.<ref name="tatekae2">{{cite web |title=Notice of Scheduled Completion of Mishima Shrine Reconstruction|author= |agency=|publisher=Mishima Shrine|url=https://www.mishimajinjya.or.jp/wp/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%86%8D%E7%94%9F%E8%A8%88%E7%94%BB/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%BB%BA%E6%9B%BF%E3%81%88%E5%AE%8C%E6%88%90%E4%BA%88%E5%AE%9A%E3%81%AE%E3%81%94%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/|date=|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> Overall completion is scheduled around August in the 8th year of Reiwa ({{ill|2026|en|2026|WD=Q49613|lt=2026|1=2026}}).

However, as the tutelary deity was too distant, a branch spirit was enshrined at the Hiyoke Inari Shrine site in Kanasukimachi of Kanasusimura. This branch is known as the Mishima Shrine of Kanasukimachi (Shimotani). To this day, the successive head priests of this shrine have been descendants of Kōno Michiyuki.

In addition, parishioners in Negishi of Kanasusimura also enshrined a branch spirit from the head shrine and co-enshrined it at Kumano Shrine; this is the Former Mishima Shrine of Negishi 1-chome.

== Shrine Grounds ==
* **Hiyoke Inari Shrine** – A subsidiary shrine ({{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}) within the grounds, serving as the land deity ({{ill|Landlord deity|en|Landlord deity|qid=Q4160872|lt=Land Deity|1=Landlord deity}}) of Mishima Shrine. Its date of establishment is unknown, but it is said that the Tokugawa clan built it within Ueno Mountain for protection against fires. In ancient times, it was known as “Misaki Inari.” Later, as the area was designated as a {{ill|Fire Protection Zone|ja|火除地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Protection Zone|1=hiyokechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566980}}, it came to be known as “Hiyoke Inari” and has since been venerated for its powers to ward off fires, bring prosperity in business, ensure family safety, prevent disasters, and secure overall safety.
* **Raijin Well** – In old Musashino, where lightning was frequent and greatly feared by the locals, lightning once struck Mishima Shrine. The priest sealed the lightning in a well. When the lightning implored, “Release me from the well,” the priest granted its request on the condition that it never strike the area again. It is said that lightning has not struck here since, and the well is revered as a talisman against lightning.
* **Mishima Shrine Hana-hyō Monument** – A monument inscribed by {{ill|Yukio Ozaki|en|Yukio Ozaki|qid=Q1152666|lt=Ozaki Kyūdō|1=Yukio Ozaki}} during his tenure as Mayor of Tokyo.
* **Survey Point** – A surveying marker established in the early Meiji era by the Geographic Division of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}}, marked by an inscription of “Fu” on the paving stone at the north gate entrance.
* Saplings of the {{ill|Miharu Takizakura|en|Miharu Takizakura|qid=Q3132219|lt=Miharu Takizakura|1=Miharu Takizakura}} in {{ill|Miharu, Fukushima|en|Miharu, Fukushima|qid=Q1348269|lt=Miharu Town|1=Miharu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.

== Reconstruction Plan ==
In proceeding with the reconstruction of the aging shrine buildings, a portion of the property will be subject to a fixed-term land lease ({{ill|Emphyteusis|en|Emphyteusis|qid=Q690076|lt=Fixed-term Land Lease|1=Emphyteusis}}) until {{ill|2099|en|2099|qid=Q972339|lt=2099|1=2099}},<ref name="tatekae1">{{cite web |title=Mitsui Fudosan’s Integrated Development Condominium with Shrine in Iriyatani |author= |agency=|publisher=Real Estate Distribution Research Institute|url=https://www.re-port.net/article/news/0000077259/|date=2024-11-11|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> and {{ill|Mitsui Fudosan Residential|ja|三井不動産レジデンシャル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Fudosan Residential|1=Mitsui Fudosan Residential|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11354219}} is developing a condominium ({{ill|Condominium|en|Condominium|qid=Q161851|lt=Condominium|1=Condominium}}). Furthermore, there is an initiative to repurpose some of the roof tiles ({{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|qid=Q268547|lt=Roof Tile|1=Roof tiles}}) and lanterns ({{ill|Lantern|ja|灯篭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12012294}}) used before reconstruction as common materials or art in the condominium.<ref name="tatekae2">{{cite web |title=Notice of Scheduled Completion of Mishima Shrine Reconstruction|author= |agency=|publisher=Mishima Shrine|url=https://www.mishimajinjya.or.jp/wp/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%86%8D%E7%94%9F%E8%A8%88%E7%94%BB/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%BB%BA%E6%9B%BF%E3%81%88%E5%AE%8C%E6%88%90%E4%BA%88%E5%AE%9A%E3%81%AE%E3%81%94%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/|date=|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> Overall completion is scheduled around August in the 8th year of Reiwa ({{ill|2026|en|2026|qid=Q49613|lt=2026|1=2026}}).

{{Source attribution|date=February 2020}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|Name=Mishima Shrine
|画像={{ill|File:三島神社(下谷).jpg|ja|三島神社(下谷).jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:三島神社(下谷).jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|所在地=Tokyo, Taito Ward, Shimotani 3-chome, 7-5, ({{sfn|東京都神社庁}})
|祭神={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
|社格=Village Shrine
|創建={{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|qid=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}4th year （{{ill|1281|en|1281|qid=Q5537|lt=1281|1=1281}}）
|例祭={{ill|June 14|en|June 14|qid=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}}・{{ill|June 15|en|June 15|qid=Q2613|lt=15|1=June 15}} on the Saturday and Sunday nearest these dates
}}

'''Mishima Shrine''' (三島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in 3-chome, {{ill|Shitaya|en|Shitaya|qid=Q11361329|lt=Shimotani|1=Shitaya}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taito Ward|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Its head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|qid=Q703904|lt=Ooyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in Imabari, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}—situated in the Ichinomiya of {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|qid=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}—which is revered as Japan’s National Guardian Shrine.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deity  
** {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ooyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
* Auxiliary Deities  
** {{ill|Watasuhiko|ja|和足彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Mihajimahime|ja|身島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihajimahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Kami-tsuhime|ja|上津姫命|lt=Thunder God|lt_ja=雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-tsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
** {{ill|Shimo-tsuhime|ja|下津姫命|lt=Dragon God|lt_ja=龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-tsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
Kami-tsuhime is identified with {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}}, and Shimo-tsuhime is identified with {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}.

== History ==
In the 4th year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|qid=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} ({{ill|1281|en|1281|qid=Q5537|lt=1281|1=1281}}), {{ill|Kōno Michiyuki|ja|河野通有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Michiyuki|1=Kawano Michiari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554275}} set out to pray for victory in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|qid=Q208156|lt=Mongol Invasion|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}} by appealing to his tutelary deity, Mishima Daimyojin ({{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|qid=Q703904|lt=Ooyamatsumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}). Under the guidance of the white heron—the divine messenger ({{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|qid=Q10444973|lt=Divine Messenger|1=Shinshi}}) of Mishima Daimyojin—he achieved remarkable military exploits. Upon his return, he received in a dream the divine command to enshrine Mishima Daimyojin in the {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}’s {{ill|Toyoshima District|ja|豊島郡 (武蔵国)|lt=Toyoshima District|lt_ja=豊島郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima District|1=Toshima district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10876926}}, and it is said that he subsequently enshrined the deity at the residence of the Kōno clan within Ueno Mountain ({{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|qid=Q746216|lt=Ueno Mountain|1=Ueno Park}}).

In the 3rd year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}} ({{ill|1650|en|1650|qid=Q6951|lt=1650|1=1650}}), he was ordered by {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} to relocate the shrine, and it was moved to Kanasusimura in Taito Ward (presently in {{ill|Negishi|ja|根岸 (台東区)|lt=Negishi|lt_ja=根岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negishi|1=Negishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11536292}}). Furthermore, in the 7th year of {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|qid=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|1710|en|1710|qid=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}}), when the shrine grounds became property of the shogunate, it was relocated to Asakusa Koayacho (currently in {{ill|Kotobukicho|ja|寿 (台東区)|lt=Kotobukicho|lt_ja=寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotobukicho|1=Kotobuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11458280}})—namely, the head shrine, Head Shrine Mishima Shrine, in Shijō-chō of Kotobukicho. However, as the tutelary deity was too distant, a branch spirit was enshrined at the Hiyoke Inari Shrine site in Kanasukimachi of Kanasusimura. This branch is known as the Mishima Shrine of Kanasukimachi (Shimotani). To this day, the successive head priests of this shrine have been descendants of Kōno Michiyuki.

In addition, parishioners in Negishi of Kanasusimura also enshrined a branch spirit from the head shrine and co-enshrined it at Kumano Shrine; this is the Former Mishima Shrine of Negishi 1-chome.

== Shrine Grounds ==
* **Hiyoke Inari Shrine** – A subsidiary shrine ({{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Setsusha|1=Setsumatsusha}}) within the grounds, serving as the land deity ({{ill|Landlord deity|en|Landlord deity|qid=Q4160872|lt=Land Deity|1=Landlord deity}}) of Mishima Shrine. Its date of establishment is unknown, but it is said that the Tokugawa clan built it within Ueno Mountain for protection against fires. In ancient times, it was known as “Misaki Inari.” Later, as the area was designated as a {{ill|Fire Protection Zone|ja|火除地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Protection Zone|1=hiyokechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566980}}, it came to be known as “Hiyoke Inari” and has since been venerated for its powers to ward off fires, bring prosperity in business, ensure family safety, prevent disasters, and secure overall safety.
* **Raijin Well** – In old Musashino, where lightning was frequent and greatly feared by the locals, lightning once struck Mishima Shrine. The priest sealed the lightning in a well. When the lightning implored, “Release me from the well,” the priest granted its request on the condition that it never strike the area again. It is said that lightning has not struck here since, and the well is revered as a talisman against lightning.
* **Mishima Shrine Hana-hyō Monument** – A monument inscribed by {{ill|Yukio Ozaki|en|Yukio Ozaki|qid=Q1152666|lt=Ozaki Kyūdō|1=Yukio Ozaki}} during his tenure as Mayor of Tokyo.
* **Survey Point** – A surveying marker established in the early Meiji era by the Geographic Division of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}}, marked by an inscription of “Fu” on the paving stone at the north gate entrance.
* Saplings of the {{ill|Miharu Takizakura|en|Miharu Takizakura|qid=Q3132219|lt=Miharu Takizakura|1=Miharu Takizakura}} in {{ill|Miharu, Fukushima|en|Miharu, Fukushima|qid=Q1348269|lt=Miharu Town|1=Miharu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.

== Reconstruction Plan ==
In proceeding with the reconstruction of the aging shrine buildings, a portion of the property will be subject to a fixed-term land lease ({{ill|Emphyteusis|en|Emphyteusis|qid=Q690076|lt=Fixed-term Land Lease|1=Emphyteusis}}) until {{ill|2099|en|2099|qid=Q972339|lt=2099|1=2099}},<ref name="tatekae1">{{cite web |title=Mitsui Fudosan’s Integrated Development Condominium with Shrine in Iriyatani |author= |agency=|publisher=Real Estate Distribution Research Institute|url=https://www.re-port.net/article/news/0000077259/|date=2024-11-11|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> and {{ill|Mitsui Fudosan Residential|ja|三井不動産レジデンシャル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsui Fudosan Residential|1=Mitsui Fudosan Residential|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11354219}} is developing a condominium ({{ill|Condominium|en|Condominium|qid=Q161851|lt=Condominium|1=Condominium}}). Furthermore, there is an initiative to repurpose some of the roof tiles ({{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|qid=Q268547|lt=Roof Tile|1=Roof tiles}}) and lanterns ({{ill|Lantern|ja|灯篭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12012294}}) used before reconstruction as common materials or art in the condominium.<ref name="tatekae2">{{cite web |title=Notice of Scheduled Completion of Mishima Shrine Reconstruction|author= |agency=|publisher=Mishima Shrine|url=https://www.mishimajinjya.or.jp/wp/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%86%8D%E7%94%9F%E8%A8%88%E7%94%BB/%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%BB%BA%E6%9B%BF%E3%81%88%E5%AE%8C%E6%88%90%E4%BA%88%E5%AE%9A%E3%81%AE%E3%81%94%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85/|date=|accessdate=2025-2-2}}</ref> Overall completion is scheduled around August in the 8th year of Reiwa ({{ill|2026|en|2026|qid=Q49613|lt=2026|1=2026}}).
